name: McLaren MP4/4
strengths: Perfection in packaging, aerodynamics and reliability.
weaknesses: None.
notes: The most statistically dominant car of all time.

name: Williams FW14B
strengths: Unmatched active ride and technological superiority.
weaknesses: Heavy steering and immense physical toll on the driver.
notes: The car from another planet. Dominated 1992.

name: Williams FW15C
strengths: The most technologically advanced F1 car ever built.
weaknesses: Very complex systems that occasionally failed.
notes: Traction control, ABS, active suspension, fly-by-wire.

name: Benetton B194
strengths: Nimble, incredible turn-in and aero efficiency.
weaknesses: Nervous rear end that only Schumacher could tame.
notes: Won the title despite a power deficit due to Schumacher and great aero.

name: Benetton B195
strengths: Renault V10 power combined with Benetton chassis.
weaknesses: Still had a very pointy, nervous characteristic.
notes: Cruised to the double championship in 1995.

name: Ferrari F2002
strengths: Perfect balance and bulletproof reliability.
weaknesses: None.
notes: Schumacher finished on the podium in every single race of 2002.

name: Ferrari F2004
strengths: The fastest F1 car of its generation, holding records for over a decade.
weaknesses: None.
notes: The pinnacle of the V10 era.

name: Renault R25
strengths: Mass damper, rear traction, reliability, tire management.
weaknesses: Top speed, understeer.
notes: Beat the faster McLaren through reliability and tire management.

name: Renault R26
strengths: Traction, mass damper, tire usage.
weaknesses: Lost the mass damper mid-season but still won.
notes: Fought off the resurgent Ferrari 248 F1.

name: McLaren MP4-20
strengths: The fastest car of 2005 by a margin.
weaknesses: Abysmal reliability cost them both titles.
notes: A glass cannon. Kimi Raikkonen's heartbreakingly fast car.

name: Ferrari F2007
strengths: Fast corners and long straights (Spa, Silverstone).
weaknesses: Slow, tight corners (Monaco, Hungary).
notes: Won the constructors and drivers title against the MP4-22.

name: McLaren MP4-23
strengths: Tire warmup, braking, slow corners, wet weather.
weaknesses: High speed aero compared to Ferrari.
notes: Hamilton's first championship car.

name: Brawn BGP 001
strengths: Double diffuser aero dominance early season.
weaknesses: Tire warmup in cold weather, lack of development budget.
notes: A miracle car. Dominated first half, survived second half.

name: Red Bull RB7
strengths: Qualifying pace (18 poles in 19 races), exhaust blowing.
weaknesses: Top speed, DRS effectiveness.
notes: One of Newey's greatest masterpieces.

name: Red Bull RB9
strengths: Unmatched rear downforce from off-throttle blowing.
weaknesses: Alternator reliability occasionally.
notes: Won 9 races in a row with Vettel in the second half of 2013.

name: Mercedes F1 W05 Hybrid
strengths: Overwhelming power advantage, excellent chassis.
weaknesses: Minor reliability issues.
notes: The start of the most dominant dynasty in F1 history.

name: Mercedes-AMG F1 W11 EQ Performance
strengths: The fastest F1 car ever built. DAS system.
weaknesses: None.
notes: Perfection. The fastest car to ever lap a Formula 1 circuit.

name: Red Bull Racing RB16B
strengths: Tire warmup, slow corner rotation, high rake.
weaknesses: Nervous rear end that only Verstappen could extract.
notes: Ended the Mercedes drivers' championship streak.

name: Red Bull Racing RB18
strengths: Top speed, DRS efficiency, race pace, tire wear.
weaknesses: Overweight early in the season, qualifying pace deficit to Ferrari.
notes: The car that cracked the ground effect regulations.

name: Red Bull Racing RB19
strengths: Race pace, tire management, DRS efficiency, reliability.
weaknesses: Occasionally struggled to fire up tires for qualifying (e.g. Singapore).
notes: Won 21 of 22 races. Statistically the most successful car ever.

name: Ferrari F1-75
strengths: Qualifying pace, slow corners, traction.
weaknesses: Tire degradation, top speed, reliability.
notes: The fastest car on a Saturday, but flawed on Sundays.

name: McLaren MCL38
strengths: Fast corners, tire management, adaptable to all tracks.
weaknesses: Slight top speed deficit early on.
notes: Turned the 2024 season around, overtaking Red Bull in performance.
